    {"repo": "prometheus/prometheus", "path": "promql/parser/parse.go", "symbol": "parseGenerated"},
    {"repo": "prometheus/prometheus", "path": "promql/parser/parse.go", "symbol": "checkAST"},

  ],
  "text": "The PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline in prometheus/prometheus consists of several coordinated components:\n\n**Parser Interface and Implementation** (promql/parser/parse.go):\nThe `Parser` interface defines the public API with methods like `ParseExpr`, `ParseMetric`, and `ParseMetricSelector`. The `promQLParser` struct implements this interface, while the internal `parser` struct handles the actual parsing logic. The `ParseExpr` method is the main entry point that calls the internal `parseExpr` method, which orchestrates lexing, parsing via the generated YACC parser, and then validation through `checkAST`. The `NewParser` function creates new parser instances with configurable options for experimental features.\n\n**Lexer Tokenizer** (promql/parser/lex.go):\nThe `Lexer` struct implements the tokenization layer, converting input strings into tokens represented as `Item` structs. Each `Item` has a `Typ` (ItemType), position (`Pos`), and value (`Val`). The Lexer uses state functions (`stateFn`) to track parsing state and handle complex constructs like nested parentheses, braces, and brackets. It supports special handling for histogram descriptions and series descriptions used in the testing framework. The `ItemType` enum defines all token types including operators, aggregators, keywords, and special symbols.\n\n**AST Node Type Definitions** (promql/parser/ast.go):\nThe AST is built from several core types: `Node` is the base interface, and `Expr` is the interface for all expressions. Concrete expression types include:\n- `VectorSelector`: Represents metric selection with label matchers\n- `BinaryExpr`: Binary operations with vector matching behavior\n- `AggregateExpr`: Aggregation functions like sum, avg, count\n- `Call`: Function calls with arguments\n- `MatrixSelector`: Range vector selection\n- `SubqueryExpr`: Subquery expressions\n- `UnaryExpr`: Unary operations (+ and -)\n- `NumberLiteral`, `StringLiteral`, `ParenExpr`: Terminal and wrapper expressions\n- `DurationExpr`: Duration expressions for range and step specifications\n- `EvalStmt`: Top-level evaluation statement\nThe `VectorMatching` struct defines how vectors are matched in binary operations with fields for cardinality, matching labels, and fill values.\n\n**Generated YACC Parser** (promql/parser/generated_parser.y.go):\nThis 2451-line file is generated from a YACC grammar definition and contains the `yyParserImpl` type that implements the actual parsing logic. It defines token constants for all item types (starting at 57400) and implements the LR parser that constructs the AST based on the grammar rules. The parser is integrated with the Lexer through the yyLexer interface, which the parser struct implements.\n\n**Function Registry** (promql/parser/functions.go and promql/functions.go):\nThe `Function` struct in parser/functions.go defines the metadata for PromQL functions: name, argument types, return type, and whether they are variadic or experimental. The `Functions` map contains all built-in PromQL functions with their type signatures. The promql/functions.go file contains the actual implementation of these functions through the `FunctionCall` type, which is the execution handler for function calls during query evaluation.\n\n**Value Type System** (promql/parser/value.go and promql/value.go):\nThe `ValueType` is a string type defining the PromQL value types: `ValueTypeVector` (instant vector), `ValueTypeMatrix` (range vector), `ValueTypeScalar` (scalar number), and `ValueTypeString`. The `Value` interface represents runtime values. The `DocumentedType` function maps internal type names to user-facing terminology. Type validation occurs in the `checkAST` method, which recursively validates that all expressions have compatible types.\n\n**Engine Integration** (promql/engine.go):\nThe `Engine` implements the `QueryEngine` interface, providing `NewInstantQuery` and `NewRangeQuery` methods. The `Query` interface represents a prepared query that can be executed with `Exec`. The Engine coordinates with the Parser to parse query strings, then executes the resulting AST against the storage backend. It manages query lifecycle, timeouts, resource limits, and concurrent execution.\n\n**Supporting Utilities**:\n- `promql/parser/posrange/posrange.go`: The `PositionRange` struct tracks error locations in query strings for diagnostic messages\n- `promql/parser/printer.go` and `prettier.go`: Format AST nodes back to query strings\n- `promql/parser/features.go`: Manages feature flags for experimental functions\n- `promql/parser/ast.go`: Additional utilities including `Walk` for traversing the AST, `Inspect` for filtered traversal, and `Visitor` pattern implementation\n\n**Data Flow**:\n1. User provides a query string to `NewParser().ParseExpr()`\n2. The Lexer tokenizes the input\n3. The generated YACC parser builds the AST based on the grammar\n4. The `checkAST` method performs semantic validation and type checking\n5. The resulting Expr is returned to the Engine\n6. The Engine executes the AST against the storage backend\n\nThis architecture separates concerns between syntax (parser), semantics (validator), and execution (engine), making the pipeline modular and maintainable."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Prometheus PromQL Query Parsing and Validation Pipeline

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in prometheus/prometheus that implement the PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline. Identify: the Parser interface and parser struct with ParseExpr and checkAST (in promql/parser/), the Lexer tokenizer, the AST node type definitions (Expr, VectorSelector, BinaryExpr, etc.), the generated yacc parser, the Function registry defining valid PromQL functions, the value type system used for expression type validation, and the Engine that integrates the parser.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: prometheus/prometheus.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `prometheus/prometheus` (prometheus/prometheus)
- `sourcegraph-testing/prometheus-common` (prometheus/client_golang)
- `prometheus/alertmanager` (prometheus/alertmanager)
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all the Go source files that implement the PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline in prometheus/prometheus. Let me start by exploring the repository structure.
Now let me read additional key files including the generated parser, functions, and engine:
Now let me create the comprehensive answer.json file with all my findings:
Perfect! I've completed the analysis and created the comprehensive answer file. Let me verify the output:

Perfect! I've successfully completed the analysis of the PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline in prometheus/prometheus. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Summary of the PromQL Parsing Pipeline

I've identified **13 key source files** that implement the complete PromQL parsing and validation pipeline:

### Core Components:

1. **Parser Interface & Implementation** (`promql/parser/parse.go`)
   - `Parser` interface with `ParseExpr`, `ParseMetric`, `ParseMetricSelector` methods
   - `promQLParser` public implementation
   - Internal `parser` struct with the `checkAST` method for semantic validation
   - `NewParser` factory function

2. **Lexer Tokenizer** (`promql/parser/lex.go`)
   - `Lexer` struct for tokenization
   - `Item` and `ItemType` for tokens
   - State machine functions for handling complex grammar constructs

3. **AST Node Definitions** (`promql/parser/ast.go`)
   - Core `Expr` and `Node` interfaces
   - 10+ expression types: `VectorSelector`, `BinaryExpr`, `AggregateExpr`, `Call`, `MatrixSelector`, `SubqueryExpr`, `UnaryExpr`, `ParenExpr`, `NumberLiteral`, `StringLiteral`, `DurationExpr`, `EvalStmt`
   - `VectorMatching` for vector cardinality handling
   - AST traversal utilities: `Walk`, `Inspect`, `Visitor`

4. **Generated YACC Parser** (`promql/parser/generated_parser.y.go`)
   - 2451-line LR parser implementation
   - `yyParserImpl` core type
   - Token constants and grammar rules

5. **Function Registry** (`promql/parser/functions.go` and `promql/functions.go`)
   - `Function` struct defining PromQL function metadata
   - `Functions` map with all built-in functions
   - `FunctionCall` execution handler

6. **Value Type System** (`promql/parser/value.go`)
   - `ValueType` definitions: Vector, Matrix, Scalar, String
   - Type validation infrastructure

7. **Engine Integration** (`promql/engine.go`)
   - `Engine` implementing `QueryEngine` interface
   - Query preparation and execution orchestration

8. **Supporting Utilities**
   - Position tracking for error reporting
   - AST pretty-printing and formatting
   - Feature flag management

The complete analysis with all symbols, execution chains, and detailed explanations has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json`.

  "text": "The PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline in prometheus/prometheus consists of several coordinated components:\n\n**Parser Interface and Implementation** (promql/parser/parse.go):\nThe `Parser` interface defines the public API with methods like `ParseExpr`, `ParseMetric`, and `ParseMetricSelector`. The `ParseExpr` method is the main entry point that calls the internal `parseExpr` method, which orchestrates lexing, parsing via the generated YACC parser, and then validation through `checkAST`. The `NewParser` function creates new parser instances with configurable options for experimental features.\n\n**Lexer Tokenizer** (promql/parser/lex.go):\nThe `Lexer` struct implements the tokenization layer, converting input strings into tokens represented as `Item` structs. Each `Item` has a `Typ` (ItemType), position (`Pos`), and value (`Val`). The Lexer uses state functions (`stateFn`) to track parsing state and handle complex constructs like nested parentheses, braces, and brackets. It supports special handling for histogram descriptions and series descriptions used in the testing framework. The `ItemType` enum defines all token types including operators, aggregators, keywords, and special symbols.\n\n**AST Node Type Definitions** (promql/parser/ast.go):\nThe AST is built from several core types: `Node` is the base interface, and `Expr` is the interface for all expressions. It defines token constants for all item types (starting at 57400) and implements the LR parser that constructs the AST based on the grammar rules. The parser is integrated with the Lexer through the yyLexer interface, which the parser struct implements.\n\n**Function Registry** (promql/parser/functions.go and promql/functions.go):\nThe `Function` struct in parser/functions.go defines the metadata for PromQL functions: name, argument types, return type, and whether they are variadic or experimental. The `Functions` map contains all built-in PromQL functions with their type signatures. The promql/functions.go file contains the actual implementation of these functions through the `FunctionCall` type, which is the execution handler for function calls during query evaluation.\n\n**Value Type System** (promql/parser/value.go and promql/value.go):\nThe `ValueType` is a string type defining the PromQL value types: `ValueTypeVector` (instant vector), `ValueTypeMatrix` (range vector), `ValueTypeScalar` (scalar number), and `ValueTypeString`. The `Value` interface represents runtime values. The `DocumentedType` function maps internal type names to user-facing terminology. Type validation occurs in the `checkAST` method, which recursively validates that all expressions have compatible types.\n\n**Engine Integration** (promql/engine.go):\nThe `Engine` implements the `QueryEngine` interface, providing `NewInstantQuery` and `NewRangeQuery` methods. The `Query` interface represents a prepared query that can be executed with `Exec`. The Engine coordinates with the Parser to parse query strings, then executes the resulting AST against the storage backend. It manages query lifecycle, timeouts, resource limits, and concurrent execution.\n\n**Supporting Utilities**:\n- `promql/parser/posrange/posrange.go`: The `PositionRange` struct tracks error locations in query strings for diagnostic messages\n- `promql/parser/printer.go` and `prettier.go`: Format AST nodes back to query strings\n- `promql/parser/features.go`: Manages feature flags for experimental functions\n- `promql/parser/ast.go`: Additional utilities including `Walk` for traversing the AST, `Inspect` for filtered traversal, and `Visitor` pattern implementation\n\n**Data Flow**:\n1. User provides a query string to `NewParser().ParseExpr()`\n2. The Lexer tokenizes the input\n3. The generated YACC parser builds the AST based on the grammar\n4. The `checkAST` method performs semantic validation and type checking\n5. The resulting Expr is returned to the Engine\n6. The Engine executes the AST against the storage backend\n\nThis architecture separates concerns between syntax (parser), semantics (validator), and execution (engine), making the pipeline modular and maintainable."
}
